text.title: Accessibility-First Design Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed when applying accessibility-first-design; each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Brand-mandated colour pair ships with 3.1:1 contrast on body text; AA failure at launch.
antipattern.root-cause: Brand reviewed colour aesthetically; contrast check ran post-implementation.
antipattern.fix: Token gate at design stage: pairs failing thresholds excluded from system; brand exceptions require a documented override + decorative-only role.

antipattern.symptom: Global `*:focus { outline: none }` strips focus indicator across the app.
antipattern.fix: Token-level :focus-visible mandatory on every interactive archetype; CSS lint forbids global outline:none.

antipattern.symptom: Parallax + auto-scroll animations play for users with vestibular disorders.
antipattern.fix: Every motion token carries a reduced-motion variant; CI lint flags raw transitions without a media-query partner.
